rationale: Hasford et al. is cited multiple times in theory-building sections, not just in a background list. The citing paper uses it to motivate the key puzzle (evidence inconsistent with a pure hedonic-contrast account), to support the claim that hedonic judgments can assimilate via priming, and to interpret Study 3 by arguing mood is not the full explanation. That is a meaningful conceptual and interpretive role in building the new attribute-hedonic model.
relation_description: The citing paper treats Hasford et al. (2015) as prior empirical support that affect from contextual stimuli can spill over to evaluations and spending for other products, underpinning its hedonic-assimilation pathway. It also leverages Hasford et al.'s mood-related evidence when discussing and ruling out alternative explanations for its own results.
